## [00:00 - 03:09]

I would help men that would not be able to talk to a woman. I would get them to be able to take numbers, updates and get the love of their life. And I did workshops at the time. But the thing is my goal in life was to make about 3 grand in euros that would be less than $4,000 a month. And that would be enough to pay for all of my expenses and everything. And at the time that I bought PLF, I was just a bit over that because I had discovered marketing. And I was making maybe six grand a month. And then I bought PLF and I said, okay, I need to bring this to the next level and see what happens. ## [15:20 - 18:28]

And at the beginning he had no list. So it was like November 21st, no list, just his daily traffic to the blog. And I said, wow, you might want to put an opt-in form somewhere on the blog. So we had an opt-in form. The first stroke of genius. The idea was, okay, a list of zero is not good. We can do better. I said, there's no way we can do worse. That's the good news. So let's see what we can do with it. So he posted an article saying, hey, I just did this podcast on how the seven ways to do XYZ. So just give me your email below and I will send it by email. And the number one thing that people said is, I want to start a business or a side business, but I procrastinate too much. So what we decided to do is try to do a program not on how to build a business, not on how to make more money, but just how to overcome procrastination. Very specialised. How to overcome procrastination when you want to start your own business. This really tiny segment of the market. And the list grew over one month, pretty much, to 300, a bit over 300 people.

## [24:34 - 27:38]

So we had a lot of trouble out of that. Maybe we are at that time. We have made a bunch of sales early in the morning. Like it's almost a six-figure launch already. But now the server is down for hours. And we have been on hours of sleep deprivation for doing because that's a takeaway. Do not write the sales letter on the night before lunch day. Been there. ## [33:42 - 36:47]

And then the salsa dance. And then, and all of this in France. And this is all, what's that? And of course there's some people here in the US that think it only works in the US. Or there's a lot of people around the world that say that it only works in the US. And the thing that struck me is when I first launched my product, the internet marketing space in France is a bit special because people in France are not as used to buying online. So the truth is when I launched my first online thing, I hadn't myself bought anything online. The first time I sold my first workshop online, I was still having a friend buy things for me and then cutting a check to them because I was putting my own credit card online. So I was like the epitome of how the French people, some of them are still scared of being online. So that was amazing to see that because we had all of this pre-launch process and I have had customers telling me that they are not confident with the whole buying something on the internet process. And because of the pre-launch content in all these markets, we were able to project to people, hey, I'm a real person, you've seen me. Even if it's just a short video because the rest of the launch is email. But just to say, hey, I'm a real person, you can email back to me, I will answer on the blog or I'll survey you on any survey service. Just that gives people enough confidence to say, okay, my money is not going out to the cyberspace and disappearing. So that was a big thing. So across all these markets and all these launches you've done, what's one of the biggest takeaways in terms of launching them, in terms of getting results? I've had some mistakes in my own launches and I've seen launches where I have been called at the last hour to try to... FIX THE DAY! I've had a friend launch a product and for the first launch day he made one sale and he said, okay, what's the problem? And when we fixed it, he made 20 sales in the same amount of time. And the thing is, there are five things and I have them on a checklist to make sure that I never made... There are just five mistakes that can be done in my opinion because people don't care if you messed up in an email or messed up a link or if the video is slow to download.

## [36:47 - 39:49]

That's not a problem. To me, the five things I've identified so far are... If a launch doesn't work out, look at the offer. Is the offer clear? Is it clear? He said, you're going to get this for this price, you're going to get it in this fashion, online or physical product and you're going to pay in this fashion. If that's clear, that's the first thing. Second thing is the offer what they wanted because you cannot... It's really hard to fail when you've asked people what they want and you've given them what they want. It's like no brainer. But sometimes I've seen people try to sell what they want to sell. And I've seen in the front space some guy with... The guy has a blog on finance and he wants to sell a product on the Zen mindset. It doesn't work that good because it's like there's a disconnect between what people want and what they sell. The third thing is the offer is really irresistible. Is it a lot of value for a small price? What I've been always pushing for is I want to give four times the value for whatever I ask being the price. I think I learned that from seeing the way you structured... When I first bought Pia, I've seen the way you structured the Pia offer. I was like, can this be worth the amount I'm paying for my business? Yes, can this be worth two or three or four times later to my business? It was worth it so that's why I bought it. So I said maybe I should do the same thing when I'm offering a product. And the other thing is... The other major mistake is... Is there a congruency for the launch? And there's a funny story, I don't know if I can talk about that. A story of a failed launch. Because when I launched my marketing strategy product in the French market, I had a few months later, two months later, one of the big guys in that market with a pretty large list launched a product by copying my launch sequence. It was like... You just took it so earlier. It was pretty much the same thing like VJV emails because I wasn't the JV list. So the JV emails, you could put them side by side and it's exactly the same thing. The bold and italics and dashes and everything is the same in the JV emails and the promotions, the video scripts are pretty much the same. The sales video was the same script, like work forward, same script. Because you had a printer, so you can actually do work forward, same script. Changing the product name.

## [39:49 - 42:51]

His name. His name, the price. And removing the guarantee on top of that. But other than that, the whole thing was just a Xerox copy of what I had done. And the guy had a huge list and on launch day he made Xero sales. And he made one sale in the night before the launch day in the next day. Before I realized because I was doing a seminar at the time and when I come back home and I look at the sales video and I'm like, I've heard this before. I've heard this before. I've said it before. So I'm like, okay, there's a problem. So I had this, the thing shut down. But the reason, in my opinion, why it didn't work is because there was no sincerity in the launch. It's like, if you see a chocolate cake and you don't know the recipe and you're not a cook, you can maybe do something that looks like a chocolate cake out of powder and paint and stuff like that. It can look the same. But since you don't have the formula, the recipe to do it, you're not going to get the same taste to it. And so that's, for me, that's a big, it's like, I'm not trying to copy paste other launches that I've seen. It's like trying to actually get a relationship with the people that are going to experience the launch. Like, okay, what do they want? And then being genuine in that relationship because that's a big part of it. People, when they are going through a launch as the prospect, they're like, okay, can I trust this person? Is this really made for, in order to help me or is this made in order to make a buck?
